channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How CIOs can survive CEO tech envy
item.description: CEOs used to think, “All IT has to do is just write a program.” It was up to the CIO to explain why, in IT-land, the word “just” really ought to be banned. Then CEO tech literacy happened.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NIS2, è scattata l’ora della compliance. Ma la corsa dei CIO non è finita
item.description: Il 28 febbraio è scattata l’ora X per la NIS2: entro questa scadenza le imprese che rientrano nel perimetro della Direttiva europea “Network and Information Security 2” (che ha aggiornato le norme dell’UE sulla cybersicurezza) si sono dovute qualificare e registrare sulla piattaforma messa a disposi
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Bubba AI, Inc. is launching Comp AI to help 100,000 startups get SOC 2 compliant by 2032
item.description: With the growing importance of security compliance for startups, more companies are seeking to achieve and maintain compliance with frameworks like SOC 2, ISO 27001 & GDPR. Bubba AI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Power Balance: MrBeast and Amazon's Deal Falls Short
item.description: The partnership between MrBeast and Amazon has come under scrutiny, with the streamer reportedly losing tens of millions on their deal. As influencers continue to try and break into Hollywood, it seems they are often left in the red.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: This fanless mini PC runs on an Intel Core i3 CPU, has two 10Gb Ethernet ports and can drive three 4K monitors
item.description: CWWK S7 mini PC features a fanless aluminum design with dual internal fans for efficient cooling, powered by the Intel Core i3-N355.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Google is working on its own version of Apple’s Hide My Email, and you might soon be able to try it yourself
item.description: Google is reportedly working on a new Shielded Email feature that will hide your email address when signing up for accounts, similar to the existing Hide My Email feature offered by Apple.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: TSMC Announces $100 Billion Investment in U.S. Chip Manufacturing
item.description: TSMC plans to spend $100 billion on chip manufacturing plants in the United States, a move that could boost the country's semiconductor industry and support President Trump's 'America First' agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Mozilla is under fire for its updated Firefox user agreement
item.description: Mozilla last week updated the Firefox user agreement — something that normally does not provoke strong reactions. This time, however, the changes led to a wave of criticism, because some of the wording can be interpreted as giving Mozilla free rein to do whatever it wants with users’ data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US Cybercom, CISA retreat in fight against Russian cyber threats: reports
item.description: Two reports published on Friday signaled the Trump administration may no longer consider Russia the US government’s top cyber foe, a development that would radically alter long-standing American global alliances to defend against nation-state threat actors in the digital realm.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: The trailer for Doctor Who's new season promises new mysteries, creepy cartoons and musical madness
item.description: New trailer for Doctor Who on BBC One and Disney Plus introduces new companion alongside time and space madness.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Giant chipmaker TSMC to spend $100B to expand chip manufacturing in US, Trump announces
item.description: Chip giant Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. plans to invest an additional $100 billion in the United States, President Donald Trump announced Monday as he appeared with the company’s head at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Michelle L. Price And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TSMC will invest $100B in US chip manufacturing
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) plans to invest $100 billion to build chip manufacturing plants in the U.S., President Trump announced Monday. The multibillion-dollar investment, first reported by The Wall Street Journal, will stretch out over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Melania Trump says it's 'heartbreaking' to watch teens grapple with the fallout from revenge porn
item.description: Melania Trump says it’s “heartbreaking” to see teens and especially girls grapple with the fallout after they’re targeted by malicious and sexually explicit online content.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: You thought genAI hallucinations were bad? Things just got so much worse
item.description: When science fiction writer Isaac Asimov published his proposed three rules of robotics (back in 1942, amazingly enough), it assumed the hard-wired instructions would be mandatory.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New malware exploits fake updates to steal data
item.description: New malware "FrigidStealer" targets Mac computers. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers four ways to stay safe from infostealer malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Bay office towers bought for far less than their prior value
item.description: The iconic Pruneyard office towers in Campbell have been bought at a huge discount to their prior value.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: The U.K.’s data protection watchdog says that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Hui, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Enterprise mobility 2025: Automation lightens the load
item.description: Enterprise mobility today is basically synonymous with unified endpoint management (UEM) software, which unifies and centralizes the management of phones, tablets, PCs, and other devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: SEC turns a new leaf with flurry of crypto probe endings
item.description: The ending of multiple Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) investigations into cryptocurrency firms is sending a strong signal that the agency’s approach to enforcement is quickly changing under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The 8 new rules of IT leadership
item.description: The CIO role continues to evolve, changing as dramatically as the technology it manages and maintains. Moreover, the pace of the chief IT position’s transformation seems to be accelerating — likewise mirroring the speed of change in the tech stack.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Soft robotic prosthetic hand uses nerve signals for more natural control
item.description: Researchers unveiled a new soft prosthetic hand that combines the natural coordination patterns of our fingers with the decoding of motoneuron activity in the spinal column.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel’s anticipated $28 billion chip factories in Ohio are delayed until 2030
item.description: Intel‘s promised $28 billion chip fabrication plants in Ohio are facing further delays, with the first factory in New Albany expected to not be completed until 2030, local media outlet The Columbus Dispatch reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: Lawrence Livermore’s Brian Spears, Judy Hill on achieving unprecedented AI advancements
item.description: Powerful computing platforms let Lawrence Livermore evolve artificial intelligence to ultimately benefit government and Americans, lab’s top AI researchers say.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government's reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former Navy comptroller shares how financial management can successfully impact decision-making
item.description: Modern financial systems offer the ideal way to tie budget to strategy and improve decision-making, explains Russ Rumbaugh, who left the Navy in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Signal will exit Sweden rather than dilute message security
item.description: The CEO of Signal said Tuesday that the service will leave Sweden rather than comply with a rule that will require vendors to capture all secure messages and save a plain text copy, in case authorities later want to subpoena that data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
